pytorch.reason: load-bearing: O(3) membership test M^T M = I and |det(M)| = 1 are verified numerically via torch.linalg operations.

pyg.reason: not used: this probe isolates O(3) shell-local properties; cross-shell coupling is deferred per four-sim-kinds doctrine.

z3.reason: load-bearing: z3 UNSAT proves no matrix can satisfy both M^T M = I and |det| != 1; the orthogonality constraint forces |det| in {-1, +1}.

cvc5.reason: not used: this probe isolates O(3) shell-local properties; cross-shell coupling is deferred per four-sim-kinds doctrine.

sympy.reason: load-bearing: sympy verifies so(3) structure constants [Li,Lj]=eps_ijk Lk and antisymmetry of generators symbolically.

clifford.reason: load-bearing: Pin(3) ⊂ Cl(3,0) provides the double cover of O(3); unit vectors in Cl(3,0) are versors; reflection v -> -n v n^{-1} gives O(3) action.

geomstats.reason: load-bearing: geomstats SpecialOrthogonal provides the Riemannian metric on SO(3) ⊂ O(3), confirming the group manifold structure.

rotation_in_O3.detail: Rotation Rz(pi/4): M^T M = I, det = +1: in SO(3) ⊂ O(3)

reflection_in_O3.detail: Reflection diag(-1,1,1): M^T M = I, det = -1: in O(3) \ SO(3)

two_components_by_det.detail: O(3) has two components: det=+1 (SO(3)) and det=-1 (improper rotations)

z3_orthogonal_forces_det_pm1.detail: z3 UNSAT: orthogonality (M^T M=I) forces det in {-1, +1} for O(1)

z3_det_bounded.detail: z3 UNSAT: no orthogonal matrix has |det| > 1

so3_bracket_L1L2.detail: [L1, L2] = L3: so(3) structure constant eps_12k = delta_3k

so3_antisymmetry.detail: L1 is antisymmetric: L + L^T = 0 (so(n) generators)

clifford_versor_reflection.detail: Pin(3) versor reflects e1 → -e1, e2 → e2 in Cl(3,0)

geomstats_so3_identity.detail: geomstats: identity belongs to SO(3) ⊂ O(3)

o3_tower_position.detail: O(3) has one parent (GL(3)) and one child (SO(3)) in the tower

scaling_not_in_O3.detail: Scaling matrix (det=2) fails M^T M = I: excluded from O(3)

identity_in_O3.detail: Identity matrix is in O(3) with det=+1

O3_closed_under_product.detail: O(3) closed under multiplication: product of two O(3) elements is in O(3)
